result:
Build is green.

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/io_lib.rs:1021` ‚Äî `f_read` (and `io_read` at :1011) returned a `TODO(port): borrow split` error. The C `g_read` holds `&mut state` and `&mut dyn LuaFileHandle` simultaneously, which the Rust borrow checker rejects since the file lives inside a `LuaUserData` owned by state.

**Fix:** Refactored the per-format read helpers (`read_line`, `read_all`, `read_chars`, `test_eof`, `read_number_bytes`) to take only `&mut dyn LuaFileHandle` and return owned `Vec<u8>` data. Rewrote `g_read` to take `&Rc<RefCell<LStream>>` and scope each I/O step in its own `borrow_mut()` block, dropping the borrow before pushing the result to the stack. Added `get_io_file_rc` for `io.read`'s default-input lookup. Wired both `io_read` and `f_read` to call `g_read` with the appropriate `first` index (1 / 2).

**New state:** Compile is green. Did not re-run the test per instructions ("Do NOT run the program at the end"). The `Runtime: TODO(port): borrow split needed for f_read` error should no longer fire; the test should progress past `print('testing i/o')` into actual I/O work. `f_seek`, `f_flush`, and `f_write` were already implemented in tree ‚Äî `io_read` and `f_read` were the next blockers.
